zh.lifeSummary: 當Constance De Léon於1185年出生在法國努維勒-阿吉泰藍德斯萊昂時，她的父親Hervé de LÉON I21歲，母親Marguerite de ROHAN22歲。她於1205年與Payen Il de MALESTROIT在法國布列塔尼莫比韓馬萊斯特洛華結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。她去世於1237年之後在法國布列塔尼莫比韓馬萊斯特洛華。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Constance De Léon于1185年出生在法国努維勒-阿吉泰藍德斯萊昂时，她的父亲Hervé de LÉON I21岁，母亲Marguerite de ROHAN22岁。她于1205年与Payen Il de MALESTROIT在法国布列塔尼莫比韓馬萊斯特洛華结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。她去世于1237年之后在法国布列塔尼莫比韓馬萊斯特洛華。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Léon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Luis, Manuel, Carlos, Pedro, Jesus, Jorge, Francisco, Rafael, Miguel, Raul.

Spanish (León): habitational name from León, a city in northwestern Spain, named with Latin legio, genitive legionis ‘legion’, a division of the Roman army. In Roman times the city was the garrison of the 7th Legion, known as the Legio Gemina. The city's name became shortened from Legion(em) to Leon(em), and in this form developed an unetymological association with the word for ‘lion’ (Spanish león).

French (Léon) and Spanish (León): from the personal name Léon, León, from Latin leo ‘lion’, or the cognate Greek leōn. The name Leo(n) was borne by numerous early Christian martyrs and thirteen popes.

variants.name: Len
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Zbigniew, Jozef, Karol.
Polish and Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): metonymic occupational name for a flax grower or merchant, from Polish len ‘flax’.
Polish (Leń): nickname from leń ‘lazybones’.

variants.name: Leonides
meanings: Hispanic (Leónides; Mexico): from the personal name Leónides, from Greek Leonidēs, itself most likely a patronymic derivative of Leōn (from leōn ‘lion’; see Leon ) + the suffix -(e)ides.

variants.name: Lyon
meanings: Scottish, English, and French: from the personal name Middle English Lyon, Old French Leon (from Latin leo ‘lion’, or the cognate Greek leōn; see Leon ). Compare Lyall .
Scottish and English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Lyons-la-Forêt in Eure, Normandy. It is unlikely to be from the better-known southern French city of Lyon (see 5 below).

English and French: nickname from Middle English lioun ‘lion’ (Old English, Latin leo), Old French leon, perhaps applied to a brave, fierce, or proud person, or one with a shaggy mane of hair. Compare Lion .

variants.name: Leos
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Pedro, Francisco, Manuel, Ruben, Carlos, Juan, Santos, Jesus, Pablo, Raul, Alfonso.
Hispanic (mainly Mexico): altered form of Basque Leoz, itself a Castilianized form (also Léoz) of a habitational name from Leotz (in Castilian: Leoz) in Navarre.
Greek: from the personal name Leos, a pet form of Leon .

variants.name: Liang
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Jian, Feng, Wen, Wei, Ping, Ying, Cheng, Guang, Jin, Jing, Liang, Chang, Chung, Hu, Nam, Pang, Jang, Min, Neng, Shen, Shiu, Sieu, Yeong.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 梁, meaning ‘roofbeam’ or ‘bridge’ in Chinese: (i) from Liang (梁), the name of a state (located in present-day Hancheng in Shaanxi Province) which was granted to Kang, the youngest son of Qinzhong (died 822  BC ), because the latter made great contributions to the fight against minority ethnic groups in western China during the reign of King Ping of Zhou (died 720  BC ). After the state was annihilated by the state of Qin in 641  BC , some people kept the name of the state as their surname. (ii) from the second element of the placenames Jie Liang (解梁), Gao Liang (高粱), and Qu Liang (曲梁), names of several cities originally within the border of the state of Jin (located mainly in present-day Shanxi province). In 645  BC , the Duke Hui of Jin (ruler of the state of Jin, died 637  BC ) ceded these cities to the state of Qin (located originally in present-day western Shaanxi province). Later, people from these cities adopted 粱 as their surname. (iii) from Liang (梁), also known as South Liang, the name of another state (located in present-day Longxi in Gansu Province) which was granted to Tang, son of King Ping of Zhou (died 720  BC ). After this state was annihilated by the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), some people kept the name of the state as their surname. (iii) from the Ba Lie Lan (拔列蘭) family, members of an ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surname to 梁 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

variants.name: Leonti
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Domenic, Salvatore.
Italian (Sicily): from the Greek personal name or nickname Leon, Leontos ‘lion’ (equivalent of Latin Leo, Leonis; see Leon 2).

variants.name: Lew
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wing, Yuen, Fook, Jung, Ying, Young, Yung, Bin, Foon, Hong, Kam, Koon.
Polish: from the personal name Lew ‘lion’, adopted as a translation of Leon .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Lev  .

nameMeanings.name: Constance
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval form of the Late Latin name Constantia, which is either a feminine derivative of Constans ( see Constant ) or an abstract noun meaning ‘constancy’. This was a popular name among the Normans, and was borne by, amongst others, the formidable Constance of Sicily ( 1158–98 ), wife of the Emperor Henry VI .

en.lifeSummary: When Constance De Léon was born in 1185, in Léon, Landes, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, France, her father, Hervé de LÉON I, was 21 and her mother, Marguerite de ROHAN, was 22. She married Payen Il de MALESTROIT in 1205, in Malestroit, Morbihan, Brittany, France. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. She died after 1237, in Malestroit, Morbihan, Brittany, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Constance De Léon est née en 1185, à Léon, Landes, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, France, son père, Hervé de LÉON I, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Marguerite de ROHAN, avait 22 ans. Elle a épousé Payen Il de MALESTROIT en 1205, à Malestroit, Morbihan, Bretagne, France. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Elle est décédée après 1237, à Malestroit, Morbihan, Bretagne, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Constance De Léon 1185 in Léon, Landes, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Hervé de LÉON I 21 und ihre Mutter Marguerite de ROHAN 22. Sie hat 1205 in Malestroit, Morbihan, Bretagne, Frankreich Payen Il de MALESTROIT geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Sie starb nach 1237 in Malestroit, Morbihan, Bretagne, Frankreich.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Constance De Léon è nata il nel 1185 nel Léon, Landes, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, Francia, sua padre, Hervé de LÉON I, aveva 21 anni e sua madre, Marguerite de ROHAN, aveva 22 anni. Lei sposato Payen Il de MALESTROIT nel 1205, nel Malestroit, Morbihan, Bretagne, Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli. Morì dopo il 1237, nel Malestroit, Morbihan, Bretagne, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Constance De Léonが1185年にフランスノウベレ-アクイティアインランズルeオンで生まれた時，父Hervé de LÉON Iは21歳，母Marguerite de ROHANは22歳であった。1205年にフランスブレタグンモービハンマレストロイトでPayen Il de MALESTROITと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼女は1237年以後にフランスブレタグンモービハンマレストロイトで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Constance De Léon(이)가 1185년 프랑스 노우벨레-아키테인 랜데스 e에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Hervé de LÉON I(은)는 21세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Marguerite de ROHAN(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 1205년 프랑스 브레타그니 모르비핸 맬일스트로이에서 Payen Il de MALESTROIT(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1237년 이후 프랑스 브레타그니 모르비핸 맬일스트로이에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Constance De Léon nasceu em 1185, em Léon, Landes, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, França, seu pai, Hervé de LÉON I, tinha 21 anos e sua mãe, Marguerite de ROHAN, tinha 22 anos. Ela casou-se com Payen Il de MALESTROIT em 1205, em Malestroit, Morbihan, Bretagne, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Ela faleceu após 1237, em Malestroit, Morbihan, Bretagne, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Constance De Léon родилась в 1185 г. в местности лeон, ландесь, ноувелле-аквитеин, Франция, её отцу по имени Hervé de LÉON I было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Marguerite de ROHAN, 22. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Payen Il de MALESTROIT в 1205 г. в местности меилестроит, морбихань, бретагн, Франция. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Она умерла после 1237 г. в местности меилестроит, морбихань, бретагн, Франция.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Constance De Léon nació en 1185, en Léon, Landes, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, Francia, su papá, Hervé de LÉON I, tenía 21 años y su mamá, Marguerite de ROHAN, tenía 22 años. Se casó con Payen Il de MALESTROIT en 1205, en Malestroit, Morbihan, Bretagne, Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos. Murió después de 1237, en Malestroit, Morbihan, Bretagne, Francia.
